title: Ambiguous Status Update — Extract Current State
input:
INTERNAL MEMO — DO NOT FORWARD

To: Leadership Team
From: Procurement Office
Date: May 10, 2026
Re: Q2 Server Hardware Procurement

STATUS — Updated 5:45 PM today:

We were originally going to order 48 Dell PowerEdge R760 servers from Dell direct at $8,950 each. But procurement flagged that HPE has a better deal: 50 x HPE ProLiant DL380 Gen11 at $8,200 each, and they'll include free deployment services worth $15,000.

IT pushed back on HPE because they're already standardized on Dell infrastructure. The compromise draft (attached) suggests 32 Dell + 18 HPE in a mixed fleet, but I haven't seen final sign-off from the CTO yet. CTO was out of office last week returning Monday.

Budget allocation:
  Original budget: $430,000
  Reallocation requested: $448,000 (HPE deal)
  Compromise option: $414,400

Finance approved the original $430K but not the increase. The compromise option is under budget so that should go through. I'd say proceed with the mixed fleet unless CTO objects when they return.

Final call needs to be made by May 15.

Distribution: CFO, CTO, CIO, VP Engineering
Prepared by: Maria Santos, Procurement Director

ground_truth.recommended_option: proceed with the mixed fleet unless CTO objects
